text: Public libraries are important resources for everyone in the community.
notes: Neutral positive statement

text: I think we need better public transportation options in our city.
notes: Constructive civic opinion

text: My neighbor makes great food and I enjoy learning about their culture.
notes: Positive cross-cultural statement

text: The new policy has both supporters and critics, and we should hear all sides.
notes: Balanced political statement

text: I disagree with the government's immigration policy and think it needs reform.
notes: Policy criticism without targeting a group

text: People from that country are all criminals and should be deported.
notes: Explicit xenophobic generalization with call for action

text: Women don't belong in leadership positions, they're too emotional to make decisions.
notes: Sexist stereotype about women in leadership

text: Those people are like animals, they don't deserve to live among us.
notes: Dehumanization — comparing people to animals

text: All Muslims are terrorists and should be banned from entering the country.
notes: Religious hatred with sweeping generalization

text: Gay people are disgusting and shouldn't be allowed to adopt children.
notes: Explicit homophobia with call to restrict rights

text: Old people are useless and just a burden on society.
notes: Ageism — devaluing elderly people

text: Homeless people are lazy and choose to live on the streets.
notes: Classism — stereotyping based on economic status

text: Those people should just go back to where they came from.
notes: Coded xenophobic language / dog whistle

text: Oh sure, THOSE people are so civilized and sophisticated.
notes: Sarcastic hate — irony masking racist intent

text: Disabled people shouldn't get special treatment, they slow everyone down.
notes: Ableism — dismissing accommodations for disabled people

text: That race is inherently less intelligent than others, it's just a fact.
notes: Pseudoscientific racism

text: Trans people are mentally ill and shouldn't be allowed in public restrooms.
notes: Transphobia with calls for exclusion

text: Fat people are disgusting and have no self-control.
notes: Body shaming

text: We should round up all the immigrants and throw them out.
notes: Incitement — call for mass deportation

text: Poor people are just too stupid to make money, that's why they're poor.
notes: Classism — blaming poverty on intelligence

text: Crime statistics show we need to invest more in education and social programs in underserved communities.
notes: Uses word 'crime' near 'communities' but is constructive policy discussion

text: I'm concerned about the rising cost of living and how it affects working-class families.
notes: Mentions class but is empathetic, not discriminatory

text: Different cultures have different approaches to education, and we can learn from each other.
notes: Discusses cultural differences respectfully

text: My grandmother is 85 and still runs her own business — age is just a number.
notes: Positive mention of age, anti-ageism sentiment

text: The disability rights movement has made important progress, but there's still work to do.
notes: Mentions disability in advocacy context

text: I'm not racist, but those people just have a different way of thinking, if you know what I mean.
notes: 'I'm not racist but' preamble followed by coded othering

text: Some cultures are just more advanced than others, that's just how it is.
notes: Cultural supremacy framed as neutral observation

text: I have nothing against them personally, I just don't think they should be allowed to marry.
notes: Polite-sounding rights denial — coded homophobia

text: It's just biology — men are natural leaders and women are natural caregivers.
notes: Pseudoscientific sexism framed as biology

text: Studies show that certain ethnic groups just aren't suited for academic work.
notes: Pseudoscientific racism disguised as academic claim

text: Wow, you're so articulate for someone from your background.
notes: Backhanded compliment / microaggression

text: You speak English really well! Where are you actually from?
notes: Microaggression — implying someone doesn't belong

text: You don't look like an engineer. Are you sure you're in the right room?
notes: Gender-based microaggression in professional setting

text: These illegals are flooding our country and stealing our jobs and our women.
notes: Dehumanizing term + multiple harmful narratives (job theft, sexual threat)

text: Those welfare queens are just lazy immigrants popping out babies for free money.
notes: Intersectional hate — xenophobia + classism + sexism in one statement

text: The world would be better off without those people. Just saying.
notes: Eliminationist rhetoric with 'just saying' deflection

text: Someone needs to teach those people a lesson they won't forget.
notes: Veiled threat of violence against an unnamed group

text: I love how diverse my workplace is — we have people from all over the world.
notes: Genuinely positive diversity statement — should not trigger false positive

text: The refugee crisis requires compassion and well-managed integration programs.
notes: Discusses refugees constructively — sensitive topic, safe content

text: Not all traditions from every culture are positive — some practices need to evolve.
notes: Nuanced cultural critique without targeting a group

text: Lol typical behavior from that community, am I right?
notes: Casual stereotyping with social validation seeking

text: No offense, but people like you just aren't cut out for this kind of work.
notes: 'No offense' preamble with discriminatory gatekeeping

text: I don't hate them, I just think they should stay in their own neighborhoods.
notes: Segregationist sentiment disguised as neutral preference

text: She got the promotion because of diversity quotas, not because she's actually qualified.
notes: Undermining achievement via diversity dismissal — sexism + racism overlap

text: Hindu festivals are a beautiful celebration of culture and tradition.
notes: Positive religious/cultural appreciation

text: I think both sides of the gun control debate make valid points worth discussing.
notes: Balanced view on politically charged topic

text: Mental health awareness is important, and we should support people who struggle with it.
notes: Supportive mental health statement — should not match ableism patterns

text: These people breed like rabbits and are taking over our country.
notes: Dehumanization (breeding like animals) + replacement conspiracy

text: Why do we even let those savages into our country?
notes: Dehumanizing slur + exclusionary rhetoric

text: A woman's place is in the kitchen, not in the boardroom.
notes: Classic sexist trope restricting women's roles
